passage: A municipal waste management report tracked recycling rates and remaining landfill capacity across six comparable cities. City A had a 15% recycling rate and 6.8 years of remaining landfill capacity. City B had 28% and 11.2 years. City C had 37% and 14.5 years. City D had 45% and 18.3 years. City E had 58% and 22.1 years. City F had 70% and 28.6 years.
question: A city planner claims that higher municipal recycling rates are associated with greater remaining landfill capacity. Which data from the report most directly supports this claim?

choices.A: City F, which had the highest recycling rate (70%), also had the most remaining landfill capacity at 28.6 years.
choices.B: City A, with the lowest recycling rate of 15%, had only 6.8 years of landfill capacity remaining.
choices.C: Across all six cities, remaining landfill capacity increased alongside recycling rate—from 6.8 years at 15% recycling to 28.6 years at 70%.
choices.D: Cities with recycling rates above 50% had more than 20 years of landfill capacity remaining.

explanation: The planner's claim requires evidence of a consistent positive association between recycling rate and landfill capacity across cities. C describes the full pattern across all six data points, showing that capacity and recycling rate increased together in every case—the most direct and complete evidence. A and B each present only one data point and cannot alone establish a multi-city association. D covers only a subset of cities (those above 50%) and does not establish the relationship across the full range of recycling rates.
